sectiondef.header: USB-C port over current signal aliases

detaileddescription.para: Identifies each eSPI logical channel supported by eSPI controller Each channel allows independent traffic, but the assignment of channel type to channel number is fixed.
Note that generic commands are not associated with any channel, so traffic over eSPI can occur if all channels are disabled or not ready

briefdescription.para: Indicates the eSPI bus was reset either via eSPI reset pin.

detaileddescription.para: eSPI drivers should convey the eSPI reset status to eSPI driver clients following eSPI specification reset pin convention: 0-eSPI bus in reset, 1-eSPI bus out-of-reset
Note: There is no need to send this callback for in-band reset.

briefdescription.para: Indicates the eSPI HW has received channel enable notification from eSPI host, once the eSPI channel is signaled as ready to the eSPI host, eSPI drivers should convey the eSPI channel ready to eSPI driver client via this event.
Indicates the eSPI HW has received a virtual wire message from eSPI host.

detaileddescription.para: eSPI drivers should convey the eSPI virtual wire latest status.

briefdescription.para: Indicates the eSPI HW has received a Out-of-band packet from eSPI host.
Indicates the eSPI HW has received a peripheral eSPI host event.

detaileddescription.para: eSPI drivers should convey the peripheral type.

briefdescription.para: Indicates the eSPI HW has received a Target Attached Flash (TAF) notification event.

detaileddescription.para: eSPI bus event to indicate events for which user can register callbacks
eSPI peripheral channel event types to signal users.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_EC_HOST_CMD.

detaileddescription.para: eSPI peripheral notification event details to indicate which peripheral trigger the eSPI callback

briefdescription.para: eSPI cycle types supported over eSPI peripheral channel
eSPI system platform signals that can be sent or received through virtual wire channel
Check if 8042 output buffer has character.
Check if 8042 input buffer has character.
Check if ACPI output buffer has character.
Check if ACPI input buffer has character.
Write character to ACPI output buffer.
Shared memory region support to return the ACPI response data.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_ACPI_SHM_REGION.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_CUSTOM_OPCODE.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_CUSTOM_OPCODE.

briefdescription.para: Get host command parameter memory size (custom)

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_CUSTOM_OPCODE.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_ESPI_PERIPHERAL_CUSTOM_OPCODE.

detaileddescription.para: Opcodes for LPC peripheral operations that are tunneled through eSPI. These opcodes are used to interact with legacy LPC devices.

memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, struct espi_callback *cb, struct espi_event espi_evt)
memberdef.name: espi_callback_handler_t

briefdescription.para: Define the application callback handler function signature.

parameterdescription.para: Device struct for the eSPI device.
Original struct espi_callback owning this handler.
event details that trigger the callback handler.

briefdescription.para: Configure operation of a eSPI controller.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to override eSPI controller capabilities.
If this eSPI controller is acting as target, the values set here will be discovered as part through the GET_CONFIGURATION command issued by the eSPI controller during initialization.
If this eSPI controller is acting as controller, the values set here will be used by eSPI controller to determine minimum common capabilities with eSPI target then send via SET_CONFIGURATION command.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
the device runtime configuration for the eSPI controller.
If successful.
General input / output error, failed to configure device.
invalid capabilities, failed to configure device.
capability not supported by eSPI target.

briefdescription.para: Query whether a logical channel is ready.

detaileddescription.para: This routine allows to check if logical channel is ready before use. Note that queries for channels not supported will always return false.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
the eSPI channel for which status is to be retrieved.
If eSPI channel is ready.
otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sends memory, I/O or message read request over eSPI.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to send a read request packet.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of structure representing a memory, I/O or message read request.
If successful.
if eSPI controller doesn't support raw packets and instead low memory transactions are handled by controller hardware directly.
General input / output error, failed to send over the bus.

briefdescription.para: Sends memory, I/O or message write request over eSPI.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to send a write request packet.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of structure representing a memory, I/O or message write request.
If successful.
if eSPI controller doesn't support raw packets and instead low memory transactions are handled by controller hardware directly.
General input / output error, failed to send over the bus.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum lpc_peripheral_opcode op, uint32_t *data)
memberdef.name: espi_read_lpc_request

briefdescription.para: Reads SOC data from a LPC peripheral with information updated over eSPI.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to read a block whose information was updated by an eSPI transaction. Reading may trigger a transaction. The eSPI packet is assembled by the HW block.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Enum representing opcode for peripheral type and read request.
Parameter to be read from to the LPC peripheral.
If successful.
if eSPI peripheral is off or not supported.
for unimplemented lpc opcode, but in range.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum lpc_peripheral_opcode op, uint32_t *data)
memberdef.name: espi_write_lpc_request

briefdescription.para: Writes data to a LPC peripheral which generates an eSPI transaction.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to write data to a block which triggers an eSPI transaction. The eSPI packet is assembled by the HW block.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Enum representing an opcode for peripheral type and write request.
Represents the parameter passed to the LPC peripheral.
If successful.
if eSPI peripheral is off or not supported.
for unimplemented lpc opcode, but in range.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum espi_vwire_signal signal, uint8_t level)
memberdef.name: espi_send_vwire

briefdescription.para: Sends system/platform signal as a virtual wire packet.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to send a virtual wire packet from target to controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
The signal to be sent to eSPI controller.
The level of signal requested. LOW (0) or HIGH (1).
If successful.
General input / output error, failed to send over the bus.
invalid signal.
timeout waiting for eSPI controller to process the VW.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum espi_vwire_signal signal, uint8_t *level)
memberdef.name: espi_receive_vwire

briefdescription.para: Retrieves level status for a signal encapsulated in a virtual wire.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides a generic interface to request a virtual wire packet from eSPI controller and retrieve the signal level.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
the signal to be requested from eSPI controller.
the level of signal requested 0b LOW, 1b HIGH.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

briefdescription.para: Sends SMBus transaction (out-of-band) packet over eSPI bus.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to encapsulate a SMBus transaction and send into packet over eSPI bus

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the packet representation of SMBus transaction.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

briefdescription.para: Receives SMBus transaction (out-of-band) packet from eSPI bus.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to receive and decode an SMBus transaction from eSPI bus

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the packet representation of SMBus transaction.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

briefdescription.para: Sends a read request packet for shared flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to send a request to read the flash component shared between the eSPI controller and eSPI targets.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of read flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by controller.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

briefdescription.para: Sends a write request packet for shared flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to send a request to write to the flash components shared between the eSPI controller and eSPI targets.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of write flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by controller.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

briefdescription.para: Sends a write request packet for shared flash.

detaileddescription.para: This routine provides an interface to send a request to erase the flash components shared between the eSPI controller and eSPI targets.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Address of the representation of erase flash transaction.
eSPI flash logical channel transactions not supported.
eSPI flash channel is not ready or disabled by controller.
General input / output error, failed request to controller.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct espi_callback *callback, espi_callback_handler_t handler, enum espi_bus_event evt_type)
memberdef.name: espi_init_callback

detaileddescription.para: Helper to initialize an espi_callback structure properly.

parameterdescription.para: A valid Application's callback structure pointer.
A valid handler function pointer.
indicates the eSPI event relevant for the handler. for VWIRE_RECEIVED event the data will indicate the new level asserted

detaileddescription.para:
dev
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
callback
A valid Application's callback structure pointer.
0 if successful, negative errno code on failure.
Callbacks may be added to the device from within a callback handler invocation, but whether they are invoked for the current eSPI event is not specified.
Note: enables to add as many callback as needed on the same device.

dev
Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
callback
A valid application's callback structure pointer.
0 if successful, negative errno code on failure.
It is explicitly permitted, within a callback handler, to remove the registration for the callback that is running, i.e. callback. Attempts to remove other registrations on the same device may result in undefined behavior, including failure to invoke callbacks that remain registered and unintended invocation of removed callbacks.
Note: enables to remove as many callbacks as added through espi_add_callback().

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Enhanced Serial Peripheral Interface (eSPI) controllers.
